stimulus:
Text 1

Scholars studying the films of Brazilian director Glauber Rocha have grappled with the sprawling nature of his work, which spans more than a dozen feature films. It is most useful, however, to consider Rocha's career as a director in three distinct periods, each with its own thematic and stylistic identity: his early experimental work in the 1960s, the politically driven films of the 1970s, and the more reflective late films from the 1980s onward.

 

Text 2

It is tempting to organize the body of Glauber Rocha's films into tidy stages. However, critics who sort Rocha's films into clear-cut phases miss possible threads that run through his entire career. Such an approach can also flatten meaningful contrasts between films made close together in time.
stem: Which choice best describes a difference in how the author of Text 1 and the author of Text 2 view the study of Rocha's films?

answerOptions.content: The author of Text 1 considers Rocha's early films to be his most innovative, whereas the author of Text 2 argues that his later films are more important.
While the author of Text 1 believes that thinking about Rocha's films in periods is helpful, the author of Text 2 considers such an approach reductive.
Although the author of Text 1 says Rocha's style changed over time, the author of Text 2 argues that his style remained mostly constant.
The author of Text 1 finds Rocha's films most powerful as political statements, whereas the author of Text 2 emphasizes their visual style.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. Text 1 advocates dividing Rocha's career into three periods; Text 2 warns that such division obscures continuities and contrasts—exactly the disagreement Choice B captures.

Choices A, C, and D introduce claims that aren't actually made in either text.
